title: The exposome in respiratory diseases: multiple preventable risk factors from early life to adulthood.
summary: Journal club review of three studies investigating environmental exposures (phthalates, air pollution, exposome profiles) and their impact on respiratory health (asthma, lung function) from early life to adulthood.
key_findings: Regulation and reduction of phthalates and air pollution are supported.
Risk reduction requires a multi-interventional approach targeting early-life factors and healthy lifestyles in adulthood.
Exposome approach highlights complex relationships between multiple environmental exposures.
study_design: Journal club review of prospective cohort studies
exposure_domain: phthalates, air pollution, green spaces, exposome
limitations: Exposome research in respiratory diseases is still in its infancy
